subject: Computer Networking
title: Starlink and Satellite Internet
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Starlink and Satellite Internet.

hook.text: Traditional satellites orbit 36,000 km up. Latency is terrible. SpaceX asked: what if satellites were much closer?

buildup.text: Starlink places thousands of small satellites in low Earth orbit — just 550 km up. Lower orbit means lower latency.

discovery.text: The constellation hands off your connection as satellites pass overhead at 27,000 km/h. Seamless and automatic.

twist.text: Starlink serves areas fiber can't reach economically. It complements city broadband rather than competing with it.

climax.text: Amazon's Kuiper and OneWeb are launching competing constellations. A new space race for internet access is underway.

punchline.text: Satellite internet went from slow backup to legitimate broadband.

quiz.question: Why does Starlink have lower latency than traditional satellite internet?
options: Its satellites orbit much closer to Earth
